final_reasoning: The image shows a road leading into a campus. A prominent sign reads 'COAST ALUMNI ASSOCIATION' and mentions the 'COLLEGE OF ENGINEERING SCIENCE & TECHNOLOGY'. The vegetation consists of eucalyptus and neem trees, which are common in South Asia. The road markings (green and white curbs) are typical of institutional campuses in India. The language on the sign is English, which is widely used for educational institutions in India. The landscape is flat with open green fields. Based on the specific name of the college (Coast Institute of Technology / Coast Alumni Association), this is located in the coastal region of Karnataka, India.
